batch_summary: On a theater rehearsal stage, a man departs, calling the play terrible. Actors Joey and Kate worry about their upcoming performance. Lauren, Joey's partner, invites him to paint mugs, but Joey declines to focus on his work. After Lauren leaves, Joey and Kate discuss their characters, Adrian and Victor. Kate suggests Victor needs to give Adrian a stronger reason to stay than just a kiss. Joey jokingly suggests "slipping her the tongue," creating a moment of silent, charged tension between them.
cumulative_summary: During a theater rehearsal, a man abruptly leaves the stage, declaring the upcoming play "terrible." Left behind, co-stars Joey and Kate express deep anxiety about their performance, which opens in just a week. Joey's partner, Lauren, arrives and invites him to go paint mugs since rehearsal ended early. Joey declines, claiming he needs to stay and work on his material. After Lauren leaves with a brief goodbye kiss, Joey turns his attention back to Kate. The two actors begin discussing the dynamics of their characters, Adrian and Victor. Kate expresses concern over a pivotal scene, arguing that Victor needs to give Adrian a compelling reason to stay rather than just a simple kiss. Joey jokingly suggests that Victor should "slip her the tongue," a comment that hangs in the air and creates a tense, lingering silence between the two co-stars.

batch_summary: Kate and Joey's intense brainstorming session quickly escalates into a passionate sexual encounter. Afterward, a thrilled Joey returns to the apartment and eagerly shares the news of their romantic breakthrough with Monica and Ross, describing how he watched Kate sleep. Meanwhile, Chandler and Rachel arrive home arguing about dating etiquette; Rachel criticizes Chandler for insincerely telling his date, Joanna, that he would call her just to avoid an awkward goodbye.
